title: Bumble founder Whitney Wolfe Herd discusses her return as CEO | Fortune

# Whitney Wolfe Herd is back as Bumble’s CEO, says she experienced complete ‘ego death,’ and is turning the dating app into the ‘Love Company’

By Emma Hinchliffe Emma Hinchliffe

Most Powerful Women Editor

By Emma Hinchliffe Emma Hinchliffe

Most Powerful Women Editor

March 26, 2025, 8:55 AM ET Add us on

Bumble founder Whitney Wolfe Herd is back as the dating app’s CEO. Courtesy of Bumble

For 14 months, Whitney Wolfe Herd was _not_ Bumble’s CEO. The founder launched the dating app a decade ago, right after she turned 25, and although she flashily [brought in new CEO Lidiane Jones](https://fortune.com/2023/11/06/bumble-whitney-wolfe-herd-new-ceo-lidiane-jones-slack/) in 2023 to herald the public company’s next era, she now says she was essentially burnt-out by then. “I had been going for about 10 years straight,” she says. “I’d been in this long-term relationship with Bumble, and it was time for a break.”
